1.label: institutional
1.description: Major established news organisations with editorial standards and fact-checking processes

2.label: official
2.description: Government bodies, heads of state, elected officials, and official state accounts

3.label: analyst
3.description: Verified journalists, credentialed policy analysts, think tanks, and subject-matter experts

4.label: commentator
4.description: Opinion influencers, partisan aggregators, and unverified political commentators

5.label: unverified
5.description: Anonymous accounts, unknown handles, parody, and automated aggregator bots

reuters.tier_notes: Global wire service with strict editorial standards
reuters.ng_criteria_notes: Wire service gold standard. Consistent corrections policy, strict news/opinion wall.

ap.tier_notes: Associated Press — wire service, fact-checked reporting
ap.ng_criteria_notes: Associated Press. Among the highest-rated organisations by all credibility indices.

apnews.ng_criteria_notes: AP News — same editorial standard as ap account.

bbc.ng_criteria_notes: British public broadcaster. Strong corrections policy, clear news/opinion separation.

bbcbreaking.ng_criteria_notes: BBC Breaking News feed — same standards as bbc.

cnn.ng_criteria_notes: Documented false/retracted stories (e.g. 2021 Zucker email story, multiple retractions). Opinion shows significantly blur with news programming. Sensationalist framing documented. Corrections policy exists but selective. ng_no_false_content based on documented NBC/fact-check records. | DATA: C3=0: 5 entries, no corrections observed. C4=1: 60% citation rate; all 5 sampled entries are reporting-framed (polls, official statements). From our data only — conflict-domain sample.

foxnews.tier_notes: Fox News — established broadcast, strong conservative editorial lean
foxnews.ng_criteria_notes: Dominion Voting Systems settlement ($787M, 2023) — network knowingly broadcast false election fraud claims. Corrections rare and slow. Prime-time opinion presented as news. Systematic misleading framing documented by Ad Fontes, MBFC, AllSides consistently. Only ng_responsible_info passes (cites sources).

ajenglish.tier_notes: Al Jazeera English — Qatar-funded, strong in MENA coverage, noted state ties
ajenglish.ng_criteria_notes: Responsible reporting with editorial standards. Known for framing bias especially on Israel-Palestine conflict and Qatar geopolitical interests. News/opinion not always clearly delimited in MENA coverage. NewsGuard-equivalent assessment: passes factual accuracy and sourcing, fails framing/opinion criteria. | DATA: C3=0: 28 entries in our data, no corrections observed. C4=1: 54% citation rate in evidence summaries; entry samples show consistent reporting-format (sourced statements, official positions).

aljazeera.ng_criteria_notes: Arabic channel — same editorial structure as ajenglish with similar framing issues.

nytimes.tier_notes: New York Times — paper of record
nytimes.ng_criteria_notes: Paper of record. Strong corrections policy. Clear news/opinion labeling. Top-rated by NewsGuard, Ad Fontes. Some criticism of framing but no systematic false content pattern.

washingtonpost.ng_criteria_notes: Strong editorial standards. Clear opinion labeling. Active corrections. NewsGuard and Ad Fontes both rate highly.

theguardian.ng_criteria_notes: Responsible and well-sourced. Strong corrections. However news/opinion distinction is frequently blurred — editorial stance permeates news coverage especially on climate and politics. This is documented consistently across media bias indices.

wsj.ng_criteria_notes: Wall Street Journal news side scores highly across all criteria. Opinion section is clearly labeled and editorially separated from news. Rated 100/100 by NewsGuard for news side.

ft.ng_criteria_notes: Financial Times. Highly rated. Strong sourcing standards, clear corrections, explicit news/opinion wall.

axios.ng_criteria_notes: 'Smart brevity' format inherently avoids sensationalist framing. Strong sourcing. Transparent corrections. Opinion clearly labeled. Rated highly by all major indices.

politico.ng_criteria_notes: Strong factual record and sourcing. However Politico Playbook and newsletters significantly blend news reporting with political commentary and insider framing. This is a consistent finding in media bias assessments.

thehill.ng_criteria_notes: Generally factual. However The Hill has significantly expanded its opinion/contributor content, making news/opinion distinction difficult. Headline framing is often sensationalist (documented by Ad Fontes).

haaretz.ng_criteria_notes: Israeli liberal daily. Strong editorial standards, clear news/opinion separation. Rated highly by international credibility indices. Strong corrections culture.

timesofisrael.tier_notes: Times of Israel — Israeli English-language news
timesofisrael.ng_criteria_notes: English-language Israeli outlet. Responsible reporting, transparent corrections. Clear opinion/news labeling.

irna_en.tier_notes: IRNA — Iranian state news agency (note: state media)
irna_en.ng_criteria_notes: IRNA is the Islamic Republic News Agency — official Iranian state news. Criterion 1 fails: documented political propaganda mixed with factual reporting. Criterion 2 passes partially (cites Iranian official sources, has editorial structure). Criteria 3-5 fail: no corrections culture for politically inconvenient errors; no news/opinion distinction; systematic deceptive framing serving state interests.

presstv.tier_notes: Press TV — Iranian state broadcaster (note: state media)
presstv.ng_criteria_notes: Iranian state broadcaster. Similar pattern to IRNA but more overt propaganda. Designated as propaganda outlet by multiple Western governments. Only ng_responsible_info partially passes (structured editorial process exists).

tass.tier_notes: TASS — Russian state news agency (note: state media)
tass.ng_criteria_notes: Russian state news agency. Documented false reporting on Ukraine, international events. No corrections culture. No news/opinion distinction. Systematic pro-Kremlin framing.

rt.tier_notes: RT — Russian state propaganda outlet, not editorially independent
rt.ng_criteria_notes: RT (Russia Today). Designated as foreign propaganda outlet (UK Ofcom ban, EU ban). Documented systematic disinformation. Fails all five criteria. Already tier 4 in registry.

dailymail.tier_notes: Daily Mail — UK tabloid, sensationalist but institutional
dailymail.ng_criteria_notes: UK tabloid. Significant record of false stories (IPSO complaints upheld regularly). High-volume corrections but driven by regulatory pressure not culture. News/opinion heavily blurred. Sensationalist headline framing is core to brand. Assessed as 'mixed' by AllSides, 'questionable' on factual reporting by MBFC.

whitehouse.ng_criteria_notes: Current administration (Trump 47). Documented false/misleading public statements. Criteria 2-5 not applicable: official accounts communicate positions, not news. ng_no_false_content=0 based on fact-check records and documented false claims. | DATA: C3=0: 29 entries, no retractions or corrections of prior statements observed. C4=0: entries present official positions as established facts throughout; no distinction between factual claims and political advocacy. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.09]

potus.ng_criteria_notes: President of the United States official account. Same assessment as whitehouse.

realdonaldtrump.ng_criteria_notes: Extensively documented false claims (PolitiFact: 71% false/mostly false during first term). Second term continuing pattern. Criteria 2-5 not applicable (official position account).

joebiden.ng_criteria_notes: Former president. Factual record generally accurate. Some misstatements but not systematic false content pattern. Criteria 2-5 not applicable.

sensanders.ng_criteria_notes: Senator Sanders. Generally factual statements. Strong sourcing when citing economic data. Criteria 2-5 not applicable (elected official account). | DATA: C3=0: 8 entries, no corrections observed. C4=0: 12% citation rate; all entries are advocacy statements ('no war with Iran', spending critiques) presented as positions, not sourced reporting. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.07]

senatormurphy.ng_criteria_notes: Senator Murphy. Generally factual. Criteria 2-5 not applicable.

netanyahu.ng_criteria_notes: Israeli PM. Documented misleading statements during conflict (e.g. Al-Ahli hospital claim Oct 2023, claims about Iranian nuclear timeline). Criteria 2-5 not applicable. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.00]

israelipm.ng_criteria_notes: Israeli PM official account — same assessment as netanyahu. | DATA: C3=0: 6 entries, no corrections observed. C4=0: entries are justifications/framings of military action; political stance presented without separation from fact. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.00]

idfspokesperson.tier_notes: IDF Spokesperson — Israeli Defense Forces official
idfspokesperson.ng_criteria_notes: IDF Spokesperson. Documented false claims during active conflict operations (Al-Ahli hospital parking lot claim, corrected retraction not issued). Military spokespersons in active combat have documented pattern of strategic misrepresentation.

idf.ng_criteria_notes: IDF official account. Same pattern as idfspokesperson.

khamenei_ir.tier_notes: Ayatollah Khamenei — Supreme Leader of Iran (official, state-directed)
khamenei_ir.ng_criteria_notes: Iran Supreme Leader. Documented false claims about Iranian nuclear programme, international events. State propaganda role. Criteria 2-5 not applicable.

iransupremeleadr.ng_criteria_notes: Iran Supreme Leader official account — same assessment as khamenei_ir.

mfa_russia.ng_criteria_notes: Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Documented systematic false claims (Ukraine war, international events). Criteria 2-5 not applicable.

mfa_pr.ng_criteria_notes: Russian MFA spokesperson (Maria Zakharova). Same pattern as mfa_russia.

narendramodi.ng_criteria_notes: Indian PM. Generally factual official statements. Criteria 2-5 not applicable. | DATA: C3=0: 7 entries, no corrections observed. C4: 0% citation rate but n=7 is too small to confirm direction — null. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.00]

un.ng_criteria_notes: United Nations official account. Factual record generally accurate. Criteria 2-5 not applicable. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.00]

antonioguterres.ng_criteria_notes: UN Secretary-General. Factual record generally accurate. Criteria 2-5 not applicable.

gavinnewsom.ng_criteria_notes: California Governor. Generally factual. Criteria 2-5 not applicable. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.00]

marionawfal.tier_notes: Mario Nawfal — widely-cited news aggregator/host, neutral but sensationalist framing
marionawfal.ng_criteria_notes: Generally accurate aggregation. Frequently links to source content. However: no public corrections issued. Heavily blends news aggregation with Mario's own commentary (no separation). Sensationalist framing — 'BREAKING' overuse, dramatic phrasing. High citation_rate in our data supports ng_responsible_info=1.

furkangozukara.tier_notes: Furkan Gözükara — AI/tech researcher, cites primary sources for conflict claims
furkangozukara.ng_criteria_notes: Consistently cites primary sources (C1, C2 well-supported by our evidence data). Non-sensationalist framing (C5). C3 and C4 set to null: our evidence logs do not constitute a formal corrections audit (C3), and the news/opinion distinction criterion was designed for institutions with editorial walls — individual analysts on X structurally blend analysis with reported facts, not a failure but also not assessable (C4). | DATA: C3=0: 49 entries, no corrections observed. C4: 41% citation rate, but sample shows mixed behavior — sourced reporting in 3/5 samples, but 'Netanyahu is panicking. He admits on camera' shows opinion stated as fact. Insufficient to assign C4 from our data.

bariweiss.tier_notes: Bari Weiss — journalist, The Free Press founder, right-center
bariweiss.ng_criteria_notes: Journalist and Free Press founder. Generally factual. Cites sources. Issues corrections. However: explicitly an opinion journalist — the Free Press blurs reporting with advocacy on culture-war issues. This is intentional, not accidental. ng_news_vs_opinion=0.

christomatos.ng_criteria_notes: Insufficient data to assess. Tier 3 based on cluster label. Criteria unassessed.

allenanalysis.tier_notes: Analysis account — frequently cited in geopolitical context, credibility unverified
allenanalysis.ng_criteria_notes: llm-classified tier. Insufficient data for criterion assessment. All criteria unassessed. | DATA: C3=0: 14 entries, no corrections observed. C4=1: 57% citation rate; summary content is sourced-fact dominant. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.00]

nathanzohrabyan.ng_criteria_notes: Regional analyst. Insufficient data for criterion assessment.

conflictnews.tier_notes: Conflict News — monitoring account, aggregates verified conflict reports
conflictnews.ng_criteria_notes: Conflict monitoring account. Consistently links to verified primary sources for each claim. Issues corrections when sources prove incorrect. Factual presentation without editorialising. Our data shows high citation_rate supporting ng_responsible_info.

sentdefender.ng_criteria_notes: Aggregator/monitoring account. Generally accurate when sources are cited. Insufficient data to assess ng_corrects_errors and ng_news_vs_opinion. ng_score withheld (< 3 criteria assessed).

jacksonhinklle.tier_notes: Jackson Hinkle — anti-establishment commentator, strong anti-US/Israel lean, frequently disputed claims
jacksonhinklle.ng_criteria_notes: Documented false claims across multiple topics including Ukraine, Iran, historical events. Assertion-heavy — our data shows low citation_rate (rarely links primary sources). Never publicly issues corrections. Opinion presented as fact throughout. Systematic misleading framing serving anti-US/anti-Israel narrative. Previously suspended from YouTube for policy violations. ng_score=0.0.

bricsinfo.tier_notes: BRICS Info — partisan pro-multipolar aggregator, no editorial standards
bricsinfo.ng_criteria_notes: Partisan pro-multipolar aggregator. Documented false/distorted claims especially on US/Western actions. Does link to sources (ng_responsible_info=1) but selection is systematically biased. No corrections culture. Opinion/news indistinguishable. Deceptive framing serving pro-Russia/China narrative is consistent.

krassenstein.ng_criteria_notes: Generally accurate factual claims — fact-checked record is relatively clean. Cites sources. Issues corrections when wrong. However: explicitly partisan anti-Trump commentary presented without opinion labeling. Framing serves political narrative even when underlying facts are accurate. | DATA: C3=0: 19 entries, no corrections observed (was C3=1 from external knowledge — overridden by data: no concrete correction example in our evidence). C4=0 retained (pure critique/commentary throughout).

edkrassen.tier_notes: Ed Krassenstein — partisan anti-Trump commentator, twin of Brian
edkrassen.ng_criteria_notes: Same assessment as krassenstein (twin, similar account pattern). | DATA: C3=0: 14 entries, no corrections observed (was C3=1 from external knowledge — overridden by data). C4=0 retained.

sahouraxo.tier_notes: Sahoura — anti-imperialist commentator, strong pro-Iran lean
sahouraxo.ng_criteria_notes: Documented false claims on Iran/Palestine conflict. Assertion-heavy — our data shows very low citation_rate and extreme stance_diversity (always one pole). No corrections. Opinion as fact. Systematic pro-Iran framing.

collinrugg.tier_notes: Collin Rugg — conservative commentator, Trending Politics
collinrugg.ng_criteria_notes: Trending Politics co-founder. Documented false claims (fact-checked by PolitiFact, Snopes). Does cite sources (ng_responsible_info=1). No corrections issued. Partisan conservative framing throughout. Misleading headline framing documented.

adamemedia.tier_notes: Unverified — possibly Adam E. Media aggregator
adamemedia.ng_criteria_notes: Unverified account. Insufficient data for criterion assessment. | DATA: C3=0: 20 entries, no corrections observed. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.02]

elonmusk.tier_notes: Elon Musk — influential opinions, not a journalist, frequent platform owner bias
elonmusk.ng_criteria_notes: Platform owner/tech billionaire. Documented false amplification of misinformation at scale. Rarely cites primary sources (assertion-heavy). No corrections issued. Opinion and 'fact' indistinguishable in posts. Systematic misleading framing documented especially on immigration, crime statistics, political content. Community Notes on his own posts at unusually high rate.

spencerhakimian.ng_criteria_notes: Unknown account. Criteria unassessed. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.00]

jvnior.ng_criteria_notes: Unknown account. Criteria unassessed. | DATA: C3=0: 38 entries, no corrections observed. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.01]

ericldaugh.ng_criteria_notes: Unknown account. | DATA: C3=0: 30 entries, no corrections observed. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.02]

shanaka86.ng_criteria_notes: Unknown account. | DATA: C3=0: 16 entries, no corrections observed. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.03]

parodyjeffx.ng_criteria_notes: Parody/satire account. | DATA: C3=0: 10 entries, no corrections observed. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.00]

hippyygoat.ng_criteria_notes: Unknown account. | DATA: C3=0: 7 entries, no corrections observed. [ng_responsible_info backfilled from citation_rate 0.00]
